general_trends: Prices for round brilliant cut (RBC) GIA-certified diamonds between 1.0ct and 1.5ct are experiencing high stability due to consistent retail interest.
Ovals and Cushion cuts are seeing a 12% surge in demand in wedding jewelry, driving slight premium increases for VVS2+ clarities.
Supply chain reports indicate a tightening of high-quality rough imports, causing polished inventory premiums for Triple Excellent rounds to rise.
market_alerts: Russian origin diamonds face increasing global import restrictions; buyers are actively favoring verified African, Canadian, or Australian source origins.
BGM (Brown, Green, Milky) tint diamonds are trading at steeper discounts (-5% to -10% below standard Rapaport) as buyers demand eye-clean white tables.
